response.justification: Candidate has 14+ years of broad software experience but the role is specifically a C++ developer position requiring deep C++/STL expertise, industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443), and IoT/MQTT background. The resume shows only ~6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments over a decade ago; the vast majority of experience is in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang on web, GIS, and ad-tech platforms. Adjacent skills (event-driven architectures, distributed systems, mentoring/workshops) are present, but the core technical fit for a senior C++ industrial-automation role is weak.
strengths: 14+ years of professional software development with strong full-stack breadth
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput, event-driven micro-services (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Demonstrated mentoring, technical communication, and workshop delivery (TDD, refactoring, Selenium courses)
concerns: Minimal C++ experience — only a brief 2014–2015 stint at National Instruments, no recent C++/STL work
No evidence of IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial cybersecurity exposure
No MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, or industrial-automation/IoT protocol experience listed

key_factors.factor: Strong overall engineering breadth and distributed-systems exposure
